original: ReviewCheckName type in src/review/types.ts accepts 'semantic' as a valid value alongside 'typecheck', 'lint', 'test', 'build'
refined: ReviewCheckName type in src/review/types.ts accepts 'semantic' as a valid value alongside 'typecheck', 'lint', 'test', 'build'

original: SemanticReviewConfig interface in src/config/runtime-types.ts has modelTier field typed as ModelTier and rules field typed as string[]
refined: SemanticReviewConfig interface in src/config/runtime-types.ts has modelTier field typed as ModelTier and rules field typed as string[]

original: ReviewConfig in src/config/runtime-types.ts has optional semantic field typed as SemanticReviewConfig
refined: ReviewConfig in src/config/runtime-types.ts has optional semantic field typed as SemanticReviewConfig

original: ReviewConfigSchema in src/config/schemas.ts validates semantic as optional object with modelTier defaulting to 'balanced' and rules defaulting to empty array
refined: ReviewConfigSchema in src/config/schemas.ts validates semantic as optional object with modelTier defaulting to 'balanced' and rules defaulting to empty array

original: When review.checks includes 'semantic' and review.semantic is omitted from user config, parsed config has semantic.modelTier === 'balanced' and semantic.rules === []
refined: When review.checks includes 'semantic' and review.semantic is omitted from user config, parsed config has semantic.modelTier === 'balanced' and semantic.rules === []

original: config-descriptions.ts has entries for review.semantic.modelTier and review.semantic.rules with descriptive text
refined: config-descriptions.ts has entries for review.semantic.modelTier and review.semantic.rules with descriptive text

original: DEFAULT_CONFIG.review.semantic equals { modelTier: 'balanced', rules: [] }
refined: DEFAULT_CONFIG.review.semantic equals { modelTier: 'balanced', rules: [] }

original: runSemanticReview() in src/review/semantic.ts accepts workdir, storyGitRef, story (with id/title/description/acceptanceCriteria), semanticConfig, and a model resolver function as parameters
refined: runSemanticReview() in src/review/semantic.ts accepts workdir, storyGitRef, story (with id/title/description/acceptanceCriteria), semanticConfig, and a model resolver function as parameters

original: runSemanticReview() calls git diff --unified=3 <storyGitRef>..HEAD to collect the diff content
refined: runSemanticReview() calls git diff --unified=3 <storyGitRef>..HEAD to collect the diff content

original: When the diff output exceeds 12288 bytes, runSemanticReview() truncates it and appends '... (truncated, showing first N files)' marker
refined: When the diff output exceeds 12288 bytes, runSemanticReview() truncates it and appends '... (truncated, showing first N files)' marker

original: When storyGitRef is undefined or empty string, runSemanticReview() returns ReviewCheckResult with success=true and output containing 'skipped: no git ref'
refined: When storyGitRef is undefined or empty string, runSemanticReview() returns ReviewCheckResult with success=true and output containing 'skipped: no git ref'

original: The LLM prompt built by runSemanticReview() includes the story title, description, all acceptance criteria as a numbered list, 5 default rules, any custom rules from semanticConfig.rules, and the diff in a code block
refined: The LLM prompt built by runSemanticReview() includes the story title, description, all acceptance criteria as a numbered list, 5 default rules, any custom rules from semanticConfig.rules, and the diff in a code block

original: runSemanticReview() parses the LLM JSON response expecting { passed: boolean, findings: [{ severity, file, line, issue, suggestion }] }
refined: runSemanticReview() parses the LLM JSON response expecting { passed: boolean, findings: [{ severity, file, line, issue, suggestion }] }

original: When the LLM returns passed=false with findings, ReviewCheckResult.success is false and output contains each finding's file, line, severity, issue, and suggestion formatted as readable text
refined: When the LLM returns passed=false with findings, ReviewCheckResult.success is false and output contains each finding's file, line, severity, issue, and suggestion formatted as readable text

original: When the LLM response is not valid JSON, runSemanticReview() logs a warning and returns ReviewCheckResult with success=true (fail-open behavior)
refined: When the LLM response is not valid JSON, runSemanticReview() logs a warning and returns ReviewCheckResult with success=true (fail-open behavior)

original: runReview() in src/review/runner.ts calls runSemanticReview() when the check name is 'semantic' instead of running a shell command via runCheck()
refined: runReview() in src/review/runner.ts calls runSemanticReview() when the check name is 'semantic' instead of running a shell command via runCheck()

original: When runSemanticReview() returns success=false, the review stage in src/pipeline/stages/review.ts populates ctx.reviewFindings with findings converted to ReviewFinding[] format
refined: When runSemanticReview() returns success=false, the review stage in src/pipeline/stages/review.ts populates ctx.reviewFindings with findings converted to ReviewFinding[] format

original: Each semantic finding maps to ReviewFinding with source set to 'semantic-review', severity from the finding, file from the finding, line from the finding, message from the finding's issue field, and ruleId set to 'semantic'
refined: Each semantic finding maps to ReviewFinding with source set to 'semantic-review', severity from the finding, file from the finding, line from the finding, message from the finding's issue field, and ruleId set to 'semantic'

original: When the agent retries after semantic review failure, the priorFailures context includes entries with stage='review' containing the semantic finding details
refined: When the agent retries after semantic review failure, the priorFailures context includes entries with stage='review' containing the semantic finding details

original: When semantic review passes (success=true), ctx.reviewFindings is not modified by the review stage
refined: When semantic review passes (success=true), ctx.reviewFindings is not modified by the review stage
